title: CLC/TS 50701 Continuous Risk-Assessment Refresh — every material change triggers fresh assessment
exclusions: Excludes changes formally documented as non-cyber-relevant. Excludes routine maintenance not changing risk profile. Excludes changes within an approved standing-risk-assessment scope.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.55.22` archived weekly to `audit_evidence` with `clc_clause="CLC-TS-50701-c11-3"`. Every row pins (change_id, zone_id, change_classified_material_at, fresh_assessment_triggered_at, fresh_assessment_signed_at, refresh_status). Dashboard `CLC/TS 50701 — Continuous Risk Refresh` shows refresh posture.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 11.3 requires continuous risk-assessment refresh, re-triggered by material change (new asset, new conduit, new threat scenario, new vulnerability class, new vendor). UC-22.55.22 instruments the re-trigger.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves every material change triggers a fresh risk assessment within SLA.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.55.22` archived to `audit_evidence`, the `CLC/TS 50701 — Continuous Risk Refresh` dashboard, and per-change refreshed assessment.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Classify a change as material; do NOT trigger fresh assessment within 30 days. UC-22.55.22 MUST flag `refresh_status="refresh_overdue"`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Trigger and sign fresh assessment within SLA; UC-22.55.22 MUST record `refresh_status="current"`.

dataSources: KV Store `rail_risk_refresh_lookup`. Material-change events from CMDB and engineering pipelines.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup rail_risk_refresh_lookup
| where change_status="material"
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_since_material = if(isnotnull(change_classified_material_at), round((now_ts - change_classified_material_at) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval refresh_status = case(
    isnull(fresh_assessment_triggered_at) AND days_since_material > 14, "refresh_not_triggered",
    isnull(fresh_assessment_signed_at) AND days_since_material > 30, "refresh_overdue",
    isnotnull(fresh_assessment_signed_at), "current",
    1=1, "in_progress")
| where refresh_status != "current" AND refresh_status != "in_progress"
| table change_id zone_id change_classified_material_at fresh_assessment_triggered_at fresh_assessment_signed_at days_since_material refresh_status
description: Tracks material changes against fresh-risk-assessment SLAs.
value: Without refresh, the risk register decays into irrelevance.
implementation: (1) Define material-change classification criteria in `rail_risk_refresh_lookup`. (2) SOAR `rail-material-change-detect` triggers on CMDB changes. (3) Schedule UC-22.55.22 weekly Monday 08:30.
visualization: (Panel 1) Refresh status. (Panel 2) Overdue queue. (Panel 3) Refresh-trigger sources.
knownFalsePositives:
1. **Multi-zone material change** — one change may need refresh in multiple zones. Suppression: tracked separately per zone.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `clc_22_55_22_exceptions` with documented Risk Manager approval and 14-day maximum grant.
reviewer: Rail Cybersecurity SME Panel + Risk Manager + Head of Engineering
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- KV Store `rail_risk_refresh_lookup`.

## Step 1 — Refresh collection

```ini
[rail_risk_refresh_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.change_id = string
field.zone_id = string
field.change_classified_material_at = number
field.change_status = string
field.fresh_assessment_triggered_at = number
field.fresh_assessment_signed_at = number
```

## Step 2 — Search and alert

```ini
[UC-22.55.22: CLC/TS 50701 Continuous Risk Refresh]
cron_schedule = 30 8 * * 1
dispatch.earliest_time = -1y@y
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.55.22,reg=CLC-TS-50701,clause=CLC-TS-50701-c11-3
action.notable.param.rule_title = Rail risk-refresh overdue
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 3 — Operationalize

**Runbook for `refresh_overdue`** (owner: Risk Manager, severity P2): trigger refresh assessment within 14 days.

## Step 4 — Troubleshooting

**CLC/TS 50701-specific failure modes**:

- **Material-change definition**: new vendor, new conduit, new threat class, new vulnerability class.
- **Continuous threat feed**: integrate threat intel for refresh triggers.
grandmaExplanation: When something big changes (new equipment, new vendor, new known weakness), we must re-check our cyber-risks. We track every big change to make sure the re-check happens; if not, the team is alerted.
